item.title: Everyone should read at least one Discworld novel
item.description: (these are the only Discworld books in English I own) Sir Terry was (and still is) my favourite author. I was introduced to his works by my cousin who gave me a polish translation of "Guards! Guards!"... a quarter of century ago.

item.title: What Happened Today #67
item.description: They’re not just pushing the limits — they’re detonating them. Trump’s machine is firing, gutting, freezing, and buying out every lever of public service that still protects people instead of punishing them. Today’s stories aren’t isolated; they’re coordinated strikes on independence, legality, and basic decency. From throwing refugees and retirees under the bus to breaking websites, defunding school lunches, and building billion-dollar detention networks, the strategy is clear: consolidate power, eliminate resistance, and make cruelty look like policy. Don’t normalize this. Fight for facts. Fight for function. Fight back. Subscribe free at https://govbrief.today #GovBriefToday #Resist 1. https://www.usatoday.com/story/news/politics/2025/04/07/supreme-court-trump-deportations-alien-enemies-act/82758348007/ 2. https://www.axios.com/2025/04/07/supreme-court-wrongfully-deported-el-salvador 3. https://www.nbcnews.com/news/world/china-vows-fight-end-trump-raises-tariffs-104-rcna200138 4. https://abcnews.go.com/US/wireStory/trump-administration-fires-senior-navy-female-officer-nato-120579606 5. https://apnews.com/article/weather-spanish-translation-noaa-nws-trump-71c727dbe2e4956247c9d9152494f1dc 6. https://www.pbs.org/newshour/world/trump-administration-terminates-some-usaid-contracts-providing-lifesaving-food-aid 7. https://www.yahoo.com/news/us-justice-dept-mobilized-armed-150244604.html? 8. https://apnews.com/article/state-department-trump-foreign-service-diplomacy-719841ea8d0fc9f75ae41e393dc0f845 9. https://www.newsweek.com/trump-confirms-iran-talks-saturday-warns-great-danger-if-they-fail-2056532 10. https://www.nbcwashington.com/news/president-trump-politics/trump-administration-president-military-parade/3886209/ 11. https://www.texastribune.org/2025/04/07/greg-abbott-special-election-sylvester-turner/ 12. https://www.nbcnews.com/health/health-news/rfk-jr-says-plans-tell-cdc-stop-recommending-fluoride-drinking-water-rcna200127 13. https://federalnewsnetwork.com/workforce/2025/04/appeals-court-restores-doge-access-to-sensitive-information-at-us-agencies/ 14. https://www.msn.com/en-us/news/us/after-trump-funding-cuts-us-catholic-church-ends-resettlement-work/ar-AA1Ctzgu 15. https://thehill.com/regulation/court-battles/5235598-trump-firings-court-ruling/ 16. https://gizmodo.com/social-security-website-crashes-blamed-on-doge-software-update-2000586092 17. https://www.nytimes.com/2025/04/07/us/politics/trump-administration-immigrant-detention-facilities-services.html 18. https://www.politico.com/news/2025/04/07/noem-offers-buyouts-to-dhs-workers-00277571 19. https://www.bangordailynews.com/2025/04/07/politics/state-politics/maine-aaron-frey-lawsuit-usda-brooke-rollins-frozen-funds-school-nutrition-children-students/

item.dc:creator: Cpthornswoggler

item.title: Book recommendations from a professional escapist
item.description:
Hi, I'm GentlemanScientist and I sell books and tabletop games for a living. Now that I've lured you in with some classic Greg Hildebrandt art, here are some of my favourite sci-fi and fantasy books. I read a lot so I've limited myself to books that I've had trouble putting down, i.e. books that I've read in one sitting, lost sleep to or gotten late to important meetings from.
Humanity once were incredibly advanced in their mastery of science, technology and nature, on the verge of engineering entire ecosystems and terraforming entire planets. Then, at the day of their most ambitious endeavour, a computer super-virus is released, instantly destroying any software it comes into contact with. Now, unknown centuries of war, strife and technological ruin, Earth is uninhabitable and what's left of humanity needs to find a new home. To that end they desperately build ark ships, slowly carrying millions of frozen people to chase rumors of these ancient terraforming projects, in hope of finding a new home. And that's only the prologue. This is fairly hard SF, no FTL, no obvious breaking any natural laws. Without spoiling too much I can say that there are aliens, and one of the best depictions of them I've seen. Their language, modes of thinking and idea history is heavily inspired by their morphology.
The Culture is an incredibly advanced society, encompassing tens of thousands of worlds, orbitals, space stations and Ships, the latter enormous world-vessels, each housing millions of inhabitants. These Ships also contain Minds, immense, benevolent AIs with mental faculties way beyond human imagination. There's no scarcity in the Culture. Everyone's needs and wants can be met. No-one is hungry, poor, in pain or miserable, unless they want to be. If you want to be a blue, half-meter high tiger-centaur, you enter a bodyshop and come out as such a short time later. If you want to design and build continents on an actual planet, go ahead! (Enter Slartibartfast jokes here.) Now, what happens when outside forces try to disrupt such a powerful and ideal society? Meet Contact, the Culture's diplomatic corps/spy agency/external affairs specialists as they take on such threats. I'm actually recommending all Culture novels here, but The Player of Games is the one that's easiest to get into, since the author explains next to nothing before throwing you head first into the story and it gives more of an overview of the society than the other books. Fascinating worldbuilding, immensely imaginative.
Ilmar. Ancient, proud, haunted, corrupt city-state. City of thousands of forgotten gods. City of bad decisions. City on the verge of a demon-fuelled industrial revolution, recently conquered by the Palleseen, an island-state obsessed with "perfection". The Palleseen intend to bring order, predictability, bureaucracy and standardisation of all things including thought, social standing and magic to the entire world. In this book we follow the clash of the Palleseen with the many diverse characters within the city, students, nobles, beggars, labor unionists, in a time where revolution is just one molotov away.
The world ended. Aliens invaded, not for conquest, but to turn Earth into a brutal, bloodthirsty intergalactic reality show, where everyday humans are thrown into an absurd RPG-inspired gladiator arena where the only real reward is survival; controlled by a not-so-stable AI. Funny, brutal, over-the-top satire of consumerism and capitalism, while at the same time entertaining as hell. I relly recommend the audio book, Jeff Hayes does an absolutely stellar job.
It calls itself Murderbot. It is a SecUnit. It is an expendable manufactured amalgamation of robot parts and cloned human tissue owned by a corporation who hires it and units like it out for personal protection. It has social anxiety. All SecUnits are sentient and intelligent. All SecUnits also has a governor module installed, ready to fry them from the inside should they show the least sign of disobedience. Murderbot has figured out how to hack its module. Murderbot is also smart enough to realise that if it shows any kind of aberrant behaviour, it will be scrapped for parts, and if it's scrapped for parts it can't watch its favourite TV shows in its head. The Murderbot Diaries is a series of cyberpunk space opera novellas, all very satisfyingly action packed while at the same time being very human with great, believable characters and a fantastic, snarky, sarcastic, anxious main character. I read the first one in one sitting and then immediately went and bought the rest. I finished three in one weekend. Coming as a TV show in the middle of May, starring Alexander Skarsgård. (PLEEEASE DON'T FUCK IT UP!)
Magic exists in the modern world. Magic also works better the more people believe in it, making somehting that's routine to cast among wizards overwhelmingly difficult in a crowd of mundanes, therefore magicians has created magical enclaves hidden from the world to keep away from ordinary people as much as possible. There are also a myriad of "maleficaria", magical beasties, that loves to eat magic and especially magician children, and they are so good at it that not one magically gifted child in twenty survives into adulthood. To combat that, in the 19th century magicians banded together and built The Scolomance, a magical academy cut off from the real world intended to teach children enough magic to defend themselves, while at the same time keeping them safe. It failed in that the maleficaria got in anyway but the survival rate improved to one in three, making it the best of many bad options. Our protagonist, Galadriel Higgins, "El" as she prefers to be called (stupid hippie mom), 13 years old, is a student at said school. As a child it was fortold that she would grow up to destroy the enclaves and wizard society. She also has an immense natural talent for dark magic, and she flat out refuses both. She is determined to survive the harsh, awful environment of the school and make her way through life regardless of what people think of her and without taking the easier path that the dark magic offers her. A fun, grim, human, more adult and frankly better version of Harry Potter. I was a bit disappointed at the ending though, but the journey was definitely worth it. Frankly, I recommend any book written by Novik, everything she's written is gold.
In a setting is highly reminescent of Victorian England, a grand island empire on the verge of industrialisation where women are relegated to wives and homemakers we follow the memoirs of Lady Trent, adventurer, scientific pioneer and natural historian, in her endeavour to overcome social stigma to follow her passion, to study dragons! I love this kind of book and wish there were more of them, the scientific exploration of something fantastic, an excavation of alien ruins, a study of a fantasy ecosystem.
Centuries ago, humanity's industrial robots gained sentience on their own, built bodies for themselves and left for the wilderness, with a parting message. "You are destroying this planet and yourselves with it. We will have no further part in doing so. Do not come looking for us." Humanity listened. They scaled everything back, disassembled and contained anything that could cause widespread harm, and eventually withdrew to a small corner of the planet, leaving the majority to grow wild. They now live in a comfortable society where technology is present, but focused on sustainability and environmentalism. If you wanna listen to music you fire up your laptop, but you have to either have enough battery power from wind or sun to do it, or you have to spend some time on the excercise bike to generate it. Dex is a bright young person who doesn't really know what to do with their life. They do reasonably well with whatever they try but doesn't feel at home with anything. They decide to try to become a tea monk, a professional listener who travels from settlement to settlement, offering a choice selection of tea, to listen, comfort and offer advice to anyone who wants it. On their travels they run into one of the ancient robots, the first ever seen since they departed. It claims to have been sent by the other robots to find out something deceptively simple, "What do people need?". Dex can't properly answer that and thus decides to bring the robot back with them, to ask more people. Also a novella, also something I read in one sitting and went to buy the sequel as soon as I finished it. So cozy, philosophical and generally feel-good.
Viv used to be a mercenary who killed monsters for rewards. Viv is a green, seven-foot musclebound orc, and very good at what she did. She is also sick of her old life and has decided to put it behind her. In a land far away, where the gnomes live, she found a beverage so fragrant, so flavoursome, wonderful, magical and completely unknown to her and anyone else from her part of the world and decides to bring it to her homeland. That beverage? Coffee. We follow Viv from when she arrives to her new home town, where she tries to find the perfect location for her passion and her new life. Warm, feel-good, cozy, with just the right amount of excitement and action. good characters, a perfect pick-me-up when you're feeling down.
I think the tagline can describe this book better than I can: "Lesbian necromancers in space". 'Nuff said.
What if you took a D&D world and then followed that to its logical conclusion? The world was full of fantastical monsters posing a danger to everyone. Adventure bands and mercenaries were hired to protect the commonfolk, slaying dragons, encountering gods and becoming legends along the way. That was then. Now, the world is mostly safe. "Adventurers" these days are more like sports teams or rock stars, travelling from arena to arena, fighting monsters bred in captivity to entertain the bloodthirsty spectators. The old adventurers are mostly forgotten, a bunch of aged has-beens. Clay Cooper, former member of Saga, the most famous band of them all is working as a city watch in a backwater town, living a slow life with his wife and young daughter when Golden Gabe, the former leader of Saga and now a loser vagabond stumbles into Clay's home trying to convince him (as he's done many times before) to get the band back together to save Gabe's daughter who's trapped in a siege on the other side of the world. "It'll be like old times, promise!". Clay promptly tells him to fuck off, but changes his mind when his daughter wonders if he wouldn't do the same for her. Fun, brutal, silly, action-packed with a huge recognition factor if you've ever played a hack'n'slash RPG. Great, clever world building and chock-full of puns, bad jokes and pop-culture references. Keep a notepad and see how many you can catch! I got a bunch more if you want them, but LATER, right now my brain is mush. Ireally hope this all posts okay, I don't wanna do this again.
item.dc:creator: GentlemanScientist

item.title: Toot toot.
item.dc:creator: TheReturnOfMadJuan

item.title: First (Stolen) ADHD memedump
item.description:
Hey y'all! Long time lurker, first time poster.
Today, at 36, I was diagnosed with ADHD combined presentation
Decided what better way to...celebrate than giving back!
After years of thinking my symptoms were flaws, I felt vindicated.
I was a "gifted" kid growing up and the oldest of 4.
So, it was easy to miss the signs in my adolescence and teen years.
But in my late twenties, I felt that these "flaws" were actually signs of something bigger.
Got laid off in November of '23, which led to me getting Medicaid.
So, I had the health insurance to see what was up.
I've been in therapy and on medication for over a year for my initial diagnosis.
Which was general anxiety and depression from trauma.
These two tools have been invaluable in my journey.
But, something still felt...off.
I have recently switched therapists, my first one retired.
So my new therapist finished her diagnosis today.
Turns out I have ADHD, combined presentation.
Meaning I'm inattentive and hyperactive, which checks out.
Getting easily distracted, careless mistakes and losing things.
Forgetfulness, difficulty organizing and procrastinating.
All of the key signs of inattentive presenting ADHD.
Interrupting others, blurting out answers without recognition and difficulty waiting in line.
Short temper and impatience, which with therapy have gotten better, thankfully.
Fidgeting, inability to sit still, feeling internally restless and EXCESSIVE talking.
All symptoms of ADHD with hyperactive presentation.
HAPPY MID-DUMP GENGAR!
I GOT'EM ALL! WOO!
Honestly, though, it does help to ease my mind and forgive myself.
I used to blame myself for all of the symptoms above, thinking I just needed to be better.
However, I had actually tried to work on every issue as an individual problem.
Instead of the megazord of symptoms that they are.
Now, I don't want this to feel like a crutch of a diagnosis and lean into it.
I want to do all of the things, i.e. therapy, medicine and other treatments, to help overcome it.
I've always felt like I've had the answers to a lot of questions, in life and school.
But, I understand the importance of asking for assistance and how it isn't a weakness.
The other reason it feels so important is, now I don't feel as guilty as I used to do.
I would procrastinate in school on projects and at work with deadlines.
I thought I was just lazy and, no matter how hard I tried, I couldn't motivate myself until the pressure.
I lose things like it's my superpower, regardless of if I throw it somewhere or purposely place it down.
I despise waiting so much, I will drive an extra 3 minutes to not sit in traffic.
I can't help but feel the urge to interrupt others during a story or conversation.
I am constantly talking to anyone or anything, including myself and inanimate objects.
I have trouble with 2 hour movies or 40 minute long TV shows, but will game for literally hours on end.
I make purchases with little thought or I convince myself I need it. (Again gotten better with age, kinda)
All of that is to say, the signs have been there looking back and I wish I could've found out earlier.
BUT, now my journey begins to try and get this under control so I can be my best self.
Not just for myself, but my wonderful wife and beautiful daughter.
Thanks for reading my story and taking in the stolen memes that have given me great pleasure recently!
I'm this picture and I don't like it.
So, have a wonderful time and seek out help if you feel like you need it!
XOXOXO Willste
item.dc:creator: Willste

item.title: Return of the Meme: 1324 (rebel magpie)
item.description:
Let's get this bread.
Seriously. I don't care where you start. Just do it, okay? It will teach you militant decency, and that is how we live through these horrors.
In case you were wondering, the supercilium is basically a stripe above the eye.
Pippin tax! Today's message from Pippin: [angry conure growls] ______ 1) Are you on the run to a safe place and passing through Arizona on your way to safer grounds? My apartment is open to you to crash at, and it is a safe space. Pippin and I will make you a meal and gladly share tea, wine, or mead with you. 2) Hey! If you're a woman or minority in a STEM field in Arizona living north of Phoenix and you need new headshots for this website or another detailing your experiences/research/accomplishment because our monstrous regime has deleted your experience from an official government website, PM me. Pippin and I have a good Nikon DSLR, and we'll do the photos for free, and, uh, you'll get to meet Pippin, I guess? (If you know someone who could use this who's not on Imgur, feel free to PM me to get my email address for them and tell them they're free to message me.) 3) Take your meds with a full glass of water, please. Do it for Pippin, or Space Mom, or better yet, do it for yourself 4) Ĝefeloað wē. We will carry on.
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: Small Stolen Meme Dump
item.description:
It's not the size of the dump that matters, but how good it feels.
I'm going through a breakup while in a dual degree law/master's program, while living in America during what appears to be the fall of American democracy, and navigating new bad brain diagnoses (expanding my collection, hooray ?)
Dude wasn't a bad guy, just kind of immature, and I've worked too hard on myself to settle for a partnership where I'm doing most of the work.
Still, leaves me in a crappy spot. We were supposed to move in together in 2 months, so now I have to find somewhere else to live pretty quick. I just kept hoping that this fight would be the one where I'd finally be able to find the right sequence of words to make him understand why I was unhappy.
I just have one more year of my program left before I finally have something to show for all this stress. Probably just gonna keep my head down pretty hard for the next year. Less distractions = better grades, maybe
Saving memes and videos that help right now so I can come back to them when I'm struggling. This is only day 4, so I'm just trying to muddle through.
Porch goose my sister made. Glad to have joined the sisterhood of weird porch geese ladies.
Benny for cat tax. He's a good boy. If you got this far, thanks for hanging out. Here's hoping, against all odds, that things get better from here. Having hope for better and getting your heart broken is better than having no hope at all. Take care of yourselves, Imgurians ❤️
item.dc:creator: andiemme
